title: Stop eating soy and beans (Genistein + Glyphosate)

chunks.title: Glyphosate and Health Risks
chunks.summary: This segment discusses the controversial role of glyphosate as a possible endocrine disruptor, highlighting studies that suggest it may promote the growth of breast cancer cells through estrogen receptors. The speaker expresses concern over the contradicting conclusions of regulatory bodies and scientific research regarding glyphosate's safety.

chunks.title: Genistein and Its Effects
chunks.summary: The segment covers genistein, a polyphenol found in soy and beans, and its potential adverse effects on human health, particularly regarding male fertility and reproductive systems. It emphasizes the negative information found in recent studies while questioning the touted benefits of these plant-based foods.

chunks.title: Critique of Plant-Based Foods
chunks.summary: The speaker critiques the prevailing narrative around the benefits of polyphenols in plant-based foods, arguing that the potential side effects may outweigh the advantages. He suggests that alternative sources of nutrients do not carry the same risks associated with these plant-derived compounds.
chunks.transcript: soy that you can't get other places with less detrimental side effects eating soy does actually really appear to be detrimental from a hormonal perspective for both men and women but at least in

chunks.title: Epidemiological Studies on Pesticides
chunks.summary: This segment presents a review of epidemiological studies linking agricultural pesticides, including glyphosate, to non-Hodgkin lymphoma. It critiques the need for further investigation into a wider variety of pesticides across different regions, especially in lower-income countries where exposure risk may vary.
chunks.transcript: i'll show one more epidemiology study with glyphosate and then move on to talking about other pesticides in relation to my favorite topic not favorite topic kale this paper non-hodgkin lymphoma occupational exposure to agricultural pesticide chemical groups active ingredients systemic review meta-analysis they say despite compelling evidence that nhl non-hodgkin's lymphoma is associated with certain chemicals this review indicates the need for investigations of a larger variety of pesticides in more geographic areas especially in low and middle income countries so you can see here that there were associations between pesticides and nhl non-hodgkin's lymphoma subtitles were reported b-cell lymphoma was positively associated with phenoxy herbicides and the organophosphorous herbicide glyphosate not all studies have showed this but some epidemiology does show this and
